{"query":"Sam Bankman-Fried Trump clemency petition legal representation filing text June 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1747,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://bitcoinmagazine.com/news/sam-bankman-fried-formally-seeks-pardon","title":"Sam Bankman-Fried Formally Files For Pardon From Trump","content":"Sam Bankman-Fried, the imprisoned founder of the collapsed cryptocurrency exchange FTX, has filed a formal clemency petition with the U.S.
Department of Justice’s Office of the Pardon Attorney, requesting a presidential pardon from President Donald Trump while serving a 25-year prison sentence for fraud and conspiracy.
“I assume that you would want a pardon from the White House?” Li asked.
Bankman-Fried was sentenced on March 28, 2024, to 25 years in federal prison after a New York jury found him guilty on all seven criminal counts in November 2023, including two counts of wire fraud and five counts of conspiracy.
Trump pardoned Silk Road founder Ross Ulbricht, former Binance CEO Changpeng “CZ” Zhao, and the co-founders of BitMEX."},{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/08/sam-bankman-fried-files-formal-request-for-presidential-pardon-.html","title":"Sam Bankman-Fried files formal request for Trump pardon","content":"Sam Bankman-Fried has formally submitted a request for a presidential pardon to President Donald Trump, according to information listed on the U.S.
The co-founder of the now defunct FTX crypto exchange is currently serving a 25-year federal prison sentence after he was found guilty of orchestrating a massive fraud scheme that misused billions of dollars in customer funds at FTX and its affiliated trading firm, Alameda Research."},{"url":"https://thenextweb.com/news/sam-bankman-fried-trump-pardon-request-ftx","title":"Sam Bankman-Fried files for Trump pardon from prison","content":"Sam Bankman-Fried has formally filed for a presidential pardon from federal prison, where he is serving 25 years for FTX fraud.
The filing, listed as a “pardon after completion of sentence,” was submitted in 2026 and is pending.
Bankman-Fried confirmed his interest in clemency during a recent interview with Fox Business from prison.
The formal application to the DOJ’s Pardon Attorney Office is a separate process from any informal lobbying.
FTX filed for bankruptcy in November 2022 after it emerged that customer deposits had been funnelled to Alameda Research to cover trading losses and fund personal ventures.
Three of his former inner circle, including Alameda CEO Caroline Ellison and FTX co-founder Gary Wang, pleaded guilty and testified against him.
Wang received no prison time."},{"url":"https://yellow.com/news/ftx-founder-bankman-fried-trump-pardon-10b","title":"Sam Bankman-Fried Petitions Trump For Pardon Over $10B FTX Downfall | Yellow","content":"Bloomberg first reported the petition, which the 34-year-old quietly filed through the Justice Department's Office of the Pardon Attorney.
A White House spokesperson and a representative for the former executive did not respond to requests for comment.
Donald Trump told the New York Times in January that he had no plans to free Bankman-Fried, even as his second-term clemency has reached dozens of white-collar defendants.
The president has already pardoned crypto figures such as Changpeng Zhao and Ross Ulbricht."}]}
